title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.

title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
summary: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.

title: Modi Urges Indians to Buy Less Gold and Skip Foreign Trips as Iran War Strains Economy
summary: Under pressure from the economic fallout of the war in Iran and politically emboldened, Prime Minister Narendra Modi asks Indians to sacrifice.

title: Narges Mohammadi, Jailed Iranian Nobel Prize Winner Is Transferred to Tehran Hospital
summary: Narges Mohammadi, a prominent human rights activist who has spent much of her adult life in and out of prison, will undergo treatment in Tehran after collapsing earlier this month, a foundation run by her family said.

title: They Were Promised New Septic Tanks. Trump Called It ‘Illegal DEI.’
summary: The Justice Department ended a deal that had helped fund a solution to the sewage crisis in rural Alabama. “Almost like we are starting all over again,” one activist said.

title: Man Charged With Assassination Attempt at Correspondents’ Dinner Pleads Not Guilty
summary: Cole Tomas Allen faces four counts in what prosecutors say was a plot to kill President Trump and other top officials at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

title: How the U.S. Is Trying to Ensure the Dollar’s Dominance During Economic Turmoil
summary: As the government has been devising plans to keep the dollar dominant, China has been making its own moves to increase global influence of the renminbi.

title: Trump to Be Joined by Elon Musk and Other CEOs at Xi Summit in China
summary: The delegation includes business leaders across a wide range of industries, including Tim Cook of Apple and Elon Musk of Tesla.

title: Drilling Into the Thwaites Glacier in Antarctica
summary: Ten people. Eight weeks. Three thousand feet to pierce a fast-melting Antarctic glacier.

title: The Voting Rights Decision Might Silence Black People in Fayette County, Tennessee
summary: Black residents, who make up 25 percent of Fayette County, Tenn., won a new electoral map last year to change an all-white board of commissioners. They worry the Supreme Court’s ruling could threaten that.

title: Mike Lawler, N.Y.’s Most At-Risk House Republican, Is Accused of Self-Dealing
summary: Several entities tied to Representative Mike Lawler, New York’s most endangered House Republican, paid a political consulting firm he once owned, raising ethics concerns.

title: U.S. Passengers Exposed to Hantavirus Begin Quarantine, Including One With Positive Test
summary: Eighteen people from a cruise ship that faced an outbreak are being monitored at medical facilities in Nebraska and Georgia. One tested positive for the Andes virus, health officials said.

title: Where Are the Passengers of the Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship Now?
summary: Nearly two dozen countries are repatriating citizens who were aboard the MV Hondius, where three passengers died in a hantavirus outbreak.

title: Inside the Elon Musk-OpenAI Trial Courtroom
summary: The tech leaders, with combined net worths exceeding $670 billion, have brought props to court and traded icy stares as their legal dispute reaches a denouement.

title: Google Says Criminal Hackers Used A.I. to Find a Major Software Flaw
summary: The company said that it had identified, for the first time, hackers using artificial intelligence to discover an unknown bug. The attempted attack represents “a taste of what’s to come,” one expert said.

title: First-Time Home Buyers Are Staying on the Sidelines as Costs Rise
summary: The effects of the war in Iran have raised mortgage rates and lowered consumer confidence, making it even harder for house hunters.

title: How Israel Turned Eurovision’s Stage Into a Soft Power Tool
summary: Israel’s efforts to influence Eurovision’s vote were broader and started years earlier than previously known.

title: How to Win Eurovision With Just a Few Hundred Voters
summary: Exclusive voting data shows that, despite Eurovision’s assurances, an Israeli campaign could easily have influenced last year’s contest.

title: Ukraine Looks to Step Away From Trump and U.S.
summary: With peace talks on ice and Ukraine now more self-reliant, President Volodymyr Zelensky seems to be stepping away from the United States.

title: Gisèle Pelicot’s Memoir Said Something Original and Taboo About Victimhood
summary: A book by the world’s most famous survivor of sexual violence has been read as a manifesto or a cry of pain. What she wrote is far more complicated.

title: In This Nazi-Era Restitution Dispute, the Focus Turns to a Missing Cow
summary: A family is vying for the return of a painting it thought was by Rubens. But an expert says it’s a copy because it does not include the artist’s depiction of a peeing cow.

title: The Silence That Meets the Rape of Palestinians
summary: Male and female Palestinians describe brutal sexual abuse at the hands of Israel’s prison guards, soldiers, settlers and interrogators.

title: I Ran the N.S.A. This Is How to Defeat China’s Hacker Army.
summary: Beijing has hacked America, but we have all the tools we need to fight back.

title: Why Two Big Companies Just Cut Paid Family Leave
summary: Deloitte and Zoom are among the employers reducing support for working parents, signaling a retreat from a “golden age of benefits.”

title: Kansas City, a Car Town, Spends Millions on World Cup Transit
summary: Security and other expenses are piling up for host cities. In the smallest metro area staging games, the bill includes temporary bus systems in two states.

title: NBC to Turn Wordle Into a TV Game Show, With Savannah Guthrie as Host
summary: The word puzzle published by The New York Times is the inspiration for a game show that will begin airing next year. Jimmy Fallon is a producer.

title: Spirit Was the Only Airline in Town. Now What?
summary: Spirit’s presence was a point of pride in Latrobe, Pa., where the security lines were short and airport parking is free. Residents are mourning the loss.
